paper.title: The origin of High-velocity stars considering the impact of the Large Magellanic Cloud

review.summary: Six internal tables: classification criteria (tab1), 9 unbound candidate parameters (tab4), GC-origin star details (tab2), HVS3 parameters (tab3), Sgr-origin HiVels (tab5), and full catalog schema (tab:catalog). One primary external resource: the China-VO machine-readable catalog (doi:10.12149/101304). Generic Gaia mission acknowledgements omitted.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Defines the classification criteria used to categorize the 519 high-velocity stars into hypervelocity stars (HVS), hyper-runaway stars (HRS), runaway stars (RS), and high-velocity halo stars based on unbound probability, disk tracking probability, azimuthal velocity, and MBH ejection origin.

columns.name: Class
columns.meaning: Classification category of the high-velocity star

columns.name: P\mathrm_ub
columns.meaning: Probability of exceeding the Galactic escape velocity curve

columns.name: P\mathrm_disk
columns.meaning: Probability of the stellar trajectory tracking back to the Galactic disk

columns.name: \nu\mathrm_\phi
columns.meaning: Azimuthal velocity in the Galactocentric frame; used with an empirical demarcation line to distinguish disk stars from halo stars

columns.name: MBH ejection origin
columns.meaning: Boolean indicator of whether the star is expected to originate from massive black hole ejection

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists the basic astrometric and kinematic parameters of the 9 unbound high-velocity star candidates identified in this work, including 7 newly identified stars and 2 previously reported by Marchetti et al. (2021) and Li et al. (2021).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Provides detailed observational and kinematic parameters for the single high-velocity star whose backward-integrated trajectory passes within 1 kpc of the Galactic Center, discussing its possible origin.

source_refs.caption: Detailed information about the star with probable origin in the center of the Galaxy. \label{tab2}
source_refs.label: tab2

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Provides updated observational and astrometric parameters for HVS3 (a previously reported high-velocity star) recalculated using Gaia DR3 proper motions, to assess its trajectory relative to the LMC.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists high-velocity stars with probable origin in the Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal galaxy (Sgr dSph), including kinematic parameters, photometry, and chemical abundances from cross-matched surveys.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Describes the column definitions, units, and semantic descriptions of the full machine-readable catalog of 519 high-velocity stars published in the online Journal and hosted on China-VO.

external_resources.description: Full machine-readable catalog of 519 high-velocity stars hosted on the China-VO platform (National Astronomical Data Center).

source_refs.context:
\label{5}

\par In this study, we identify a total of 519 high-velocity stars (HiVels) through a cross-matching process between the \textit{Gaia} DR3 and other Large-scale Galactic Surveys.  A catalog of the properties of these stars is available on China-VO: \dataset[doi:10.12149/101304]{https://nadc.china-vo.org/res/r101304/}. These HiVels consist of 9 unbound candidates under the {\tt\string Gala} {\tt\string MilkyWayPotential}, 57 runaway stars (not the typical OB type runaway stars), and 453 high-velocity halo stars. In our sample, 78 HiVels have been reported by \citet{Li.Yinbi2021}, a candidate HiVel originating from the Sgr dSph has been reported by \citet{Lihf2022}, and 440 have been reported for the first time.

These HiVels consist of 9 unbound candidates under the {\tt\string Gala} {\tt\string MilkyWayPotential}, 57 runaway stars (not the typical OB type runaway stars), and 453 high-velocity halo stars. In our sample, 78 HiVels have been reported by \citet{Li.Yinbi2021}, a candidate HiVel originating from the Sgr dSph has been reported by \citet{Lihf2022}, and 440 have been reported for the first time.
